title: Human Dialer — Power & Predictive Dialing for Your Team
lede: Turn a list of leads into back-to-back live conversations. The Human Dialer places outbound calls automatically, screens out voicemails with AI, and rings only the real humans through to your sales team — so your people spend their day talking, not dialing.

steps.title: Meet the Human Dialer
steps.content:
The Human Dialer is the counterpart to your AI campaigns: instead of an AI agent doing the talking, your human team takes the calls — and the platform does everything else. It dials the list, detects answering machines, and hands each connected human to the first available agent, right in their web dialer.

Two dialing modes are built in:

  • 📞 Power Dial — one line per agent. The moment an agent is free, the next contact is dialed. Zero risk of abandoned calls.
  • ⚡ Predictive — several lines per agent at once, screened by AI. Maximum talk time for large teams, with a configurable overflow policy for the rare call nobody can take.
  1. In the top navigation, click Campaigns.
  2. Select the Human Dialer sub-tab. You land on the Dial Lists table — every human-dialing campaign you have.
  3. Each row shows the list's Name, Description, Contacts count, Progress bar (completed vs. total), Status (Active/Inactive) and Created date. Use the Search dial lists box or the Filter by owner dropdown to find a list fast.
  4. Under Actions, the green eye opens a list and the red trash icon deletes it (you'll be asked to confirm — deleting also removes the list's queued contacts).
💡 Looking for AI-run campaigns instead? That's the Smart Dialer tab right next door — the Human Dialer is specifically for lists your people will talk through live.

images.alt: Human Dialer dial lists table under Campaigns
images.caption: Campaigns → Human Dialer — every dial list with contacts, progress, and status at a glance.

images.alt: Human Dialer dial lists table under Campaigns (dark mode)
images.caption: Campaigns → Human Dialer — every dial list with contacts, progress, and status at a glance.

steps.title: Create your dial list
steps.content:
A dial list is the container for one human-dialing campaign: the people to call plus the dialer configuration.

  1. Click Create Dial List in the top-right of the Human Dialer page.
  2. Enter a Name your team will recognize (required) — e.g. "Warm Leads Follow-Up".
  3. Optionally add a Description so everyone knows what this list is for.
  4. Leave the Active toggle on (an inactive list is kept but clearly flagged as not in use).
  5. Click Save. You're taken straight to the new list's page.

images.alt: Create Dial List form with Name, Description and Active toggle
images.caption: Create Dial List — a name is all you need to get started.

images.alt: Create Dial List form with Name, Description and Active toggle (dark mode)
images.caption: Create Dial List — a name is all you need to get started.

steps.title: Load contacts and leads into the queue
steps.content:
Now fill the list with the people your team should reach. Everything comes straight from your CRM — no CSV juggling.

  1. On the dial list page, click Add Contacts/Leads in the top-right.
  2. In the modal, pick the Entity Type: Contacts or Leads.
  3. Narrow the pool with the three filters: Search (by name or email), Filter by Tag, and Filter by Account.
  4. Tick individual people, or use Select All on This Page. For big audiences, Select All Across All Pages grabs every record that matches your filters — the total count is shown right on the checkbox.
  5. Click Add Selected. Large selections show a progress dialog while they're queued.
💡 You can re-open this modal any time to top up the list — people already on the list aren't duplicated, and the dialer picks up new entries even mid-session.

images.alt: Add Contacts/Leads to Dial List modal with entity type, search, tag and account filters
images.caption: Add Contacts/Leads — filter your CRM, select in bulk, done.

images.alt: Add Contacts/Leads to Dial List modal with entity type, search, tag and account filters (dark mode)
images.caption: Add Contacts/Leads — filter your CRM, select in bulk, done.

steps.title: Know your way around the dial list page
steps.content:
The list page is mission control for this campaign. From top to bottom:

  • Stat tiles — Total Contacts, Completed, Progress (%), and Status (Active/Inactive).
  • Predictive Dialer card — the heart of the page. It shows the current configuration as chips (or Not configured yet), with Configure and Start Session buttons. While a session runs, this card becomes the live dashboard.
  • Description card — click Edit to update the note for your team.
  • Contacts card — the full calling queue (next step).

Two handy details:

  1. Click the list's title (or the ✏️ pencil next to it) to rename it inline.
  2. Notice the hint under the buttons: agents never need to open this page — they take calls from their own web dialer. This page is for whoever runs the campaign.

images.alt: Dial list page with stat tiles, Predictive Dialer card, description and contacts
images.caption: The dial list page — stats on top, the Predictive Dialer card in the middle, the queue below.

images.alt: Dial list page with stat tiles, Predictive Dialer card, description and contacts (dark mode)
images.caption: The dial list page — stats on top, the Predictive Dialer card in the middle, the queue below.

steps.title: Manage the calling queue
steps.content:
The Contacts card lists everyone in the queue with their live calling state:

  • Name — with a lead/contact badge; click through to the CRM record.
  • Phone Number — the number that will be dialed.
  • Status — e.g. pending (not called yet) or completed.
  • Lifecycle Stage — the CRM stage, used by the dialer's audience filters.
  • Call Count and Last Called — how many attempts so far and when the last one was.
  1. Click Filters (top-right of the card) to open the filter panel: Status, Lifecycle Stage, Call Count (Min) and Call Count (Max). Clear Filters resets everything.
  2. To dial one person manually right now, click the blue phone icon in their Actions column — it opens your web dialer with the number ready.
  3. To prune the queue, tick rows (or use Select All for every page) and click Remove Selected. The red trash icon removes a single entry.
⚠️ Removing someone from the dial list only takes them out of this queue — it never deletes the contact or lead from your CRM.

images.alt: Contacts queue table with filters, statuses, call counts and per-row actions
images.caption: The queue — filter it, dial one manually, or remove entries in bulk.

images.alt: Contacts queue table with filters, statuses, call counts and per-row actions (dark mode)
images.caption: The queue — filter it, dial one manually, or remove entries in bulk.

steps.title: Configure the dialer — mode, screening, and routing
steps.content:
Click Configure on the Predictive Dialer card to open the Dialer Settings drawer. This is where the magic is tuned. Top to bottom:

  • Dialing mode — pick the 📞 Power Dial card (1 line per agent, no abandoned calls) or ⚡ Predictive (multiple lines per agent, AI-screened, maximum talk time).
  • Lines per agent (Predictive only) — 2, 3, 4, 5, 8 or 10 simultaneous calls per ready agent. Higher = less waiting, but more chance a human answers when everyone is busy.
  • Use AMD screening — on: the AI listens to who answers and only hands humans to your team (voicemails are dropped automatically). Off: the call connects to the group the instant it's answered, voicemails included.
  • Screening AI agent — the assistant that does the listening. Any transcription-only assistant works; the dialer runs answering-machine detection on it automatically.
  • Route calls to group — required. Screened calls ring this Contact Center group's online agents, first to accept wins. The dialer paces itself off how many are available — and won't dial at all when nobody's online.
  • Exclude specific agents (optional) — group members who should not receive calls from this campaign.
  • Your caller ID(s) — pick one or more of your phone numbers. With several selected, the dialer rotates them round-robin, one per call, to spread volume.
  • Route callbacks to the group — while the campaign runs, a prospect calling back one of your caller IDs rings the same group. The dropdown below sets the fallback when nobody's free: Let the number's default AI agent answer, Hand to a specific AI agent, Send to voicemail, or Drop the call.
  • Overflow policy (Predictive only) — when a human answers but every agent is busy: Hang up politely or Hand to AI assistant (pick which one). Overflow-to-AI means no opportunity is ever wasted.
  • Wrap-up time (seconds) — breathing room after each call before the next one is pushed to the agent.
  • Treat uncertain answers as human — when the AI can't tell human from machine, connect it to an agent anyway (recommended: better a rare voicemail than a missed prospect).
  • Dialing order — List order (as added), Fewest attempts first, Longest since last call first, or Shuffle.

The footer always shows Eligible to dial: X of Y — a live preview of how many queued contacts pass your current rules. Click Save Settings when you're done.

⚠️ Your caller ID must be a number with outbound calling enabled — the save will tell you if a selected number has no outbound trunk configured. Settings are also locked while a session is running: stop the session first.

images.alt: Dialer Settings drawer with mode cards, screening, group routing, caller IDs and overflow policy
images.caption: Dialer Settings — mode, AI screening, the operator group, caller IDs, callbacks and overflow.

images.alt: Dialer Settings drawer with mode cards, screening, group routing, caller IDs and overflow policy (dark mode)
images.caption: Dialer Settings — mode, AI screening, the operator group, caller IDs, callbacks and overflow.

steps.title: Fine-tune retries, audience, and calling hours
steps.content:
Below the main settings, four collapsible panels give you enterprise-grade control:

  • 🔄 Retry policy — Stop retrying after N total attempt(s) per contact; Minimum wait between attempts (in minutes, hours or days) so nobody gets hammered; Limit attempts per day to N per contact; and Retry only these outcomes — re-dial only contacts whose last call ended a certain way (no answer, busy, voicemail…). Never-called contacts are always eligible.
  • 🔎 Who gets dialed — include or exclude lifecycle stages, require or exclude tags, and add custom field filters (each row must match the contact's custom field exactly, case-insensitive). Perfect for dialing only "New" leads tagged "VIP".
  • 🕐 Calling hours — flip on Only dial during these hours, then set the window, the Days, and the Timezone. Outside the window no new calls are placed — calls already ringing finish normally, and dialing resumes by itself.
  • 🏷️ Custom lifecycle stages — give this list its own stage set (it starts as a copy of your CRM stages). These stages power the filters above and the post-call status popup your agents see.
💡 The Eligible to dial counter in the drawer footer updates live as you tighten these rules — you always know exactly how big your dialable audience is before you hit start.

images.alt: Advanced panels: Retry policy, Who gets dialed, Calling hours, Custom lifecycle stages
images.caption: Retry limits, audience filters, calling hours and custom stages — all per dial list.

images.alt: Advanced panels: Retry policy, Who gets dialed, Calling hours, Custom lifecycle stages (dark mode)
images.caption: Retry limits, audience filters, calling hours and custom stages — all per dial list.

steps.title: Start the session and watch it live
steps.content:
Once configured, the Predictive Dialer card summarizes everything as chips — mode, lines per agent, screening agent, caller ID, operator group, overflow policy, wrap-up time, and how many contacts are eligible.

  1. Make sure at least one agent in the routed group is online and Available in their web dialer (next step) — the dialer only places calls when someone can take them.
  2. Click Start Session. Only the main account can start, pause or stop sessions.
  3. The card flips into a live dashboard: a Running badge, live counters for Connects (with humans reached), Voicemails (and total dialed), Abandoned (plus failed/cancelled), and AI Handoffs, a queue progress bar, and a roster of your agents with their current state.
  4. Use Pause / Resume to hold dialing without losing your place, and Stop to end the session — you'll be asked to confirm, and any active lines are hung up.
💡 The session is fully server-side: you can close this page, and dialing continues. Come back any time — the dashboard reconnects to the live session automatically.

images.alt: Predictive Dialer launcher card with Configure and Start Session buttons
images.caption: The launcher card — configure once, then Start Session. While running, this becomes the live board.

images.alt: Predictive Dialer launcher card with Configure and Start Session buttons (dark mode)
images.caption: The launcher card — configure once, then Start Session. While running, this becomes the live board.

steps.title: What your agents do — just be Available
steps.content:
Here's the beautiful part: your agents don't open the campaign at all. Their entire world is the web dialer they already use for inbound calls.

  1. Each agent logs into their own iMash account and opens the web dialer (the phone icon in the top-right of any page).
  2. In the My status panel at the bottom of the dialer, they set themselves to Online. That's it — they're now in the pool for every campaign routed to their group.
  3. When the dialer connects a real human, the call rings in their web dialer like any inbound call — with the contact's name and campaign context on the ring card. First agent to accept wins the call.
  4. After hanging up, a short wrap-up countdown runs (the one you configured) before the next call is pushed. A status popup lets them log the outcome and move the contact's stage.
💡 Because screened calls ring the whole group and the first to accept wins, adding capacity is as simple as more agents flipping themselves Online — the dialer instantly paces up to match.

images.alt: Agent view with the web dialer open on the right side
images.caption: The agent's view — the web dialer is where campaign calls arrive.

images.alt: Agent view with the web dialer open on the right side (dark mode)
images.caption: The agent's view — the web dialer is where campaign calls arrive.

images.alt: My status panel in the web dialer set to Online
images.caption: My status → Online — the only switch an agent needs to flip.

images.alt: My status panel in the web dialer set to Online (dark mode)
images.caption: My status → Online — the only switch an agent needs to flip.

steps.title: Review the results
steps.content:
Every dialer call is a first-class call in iMash, so your usual reporting works out of the box:

  • The dial list's Progress tile and each contact's Status, Call Count and Last Called update as the session runs.
  • Open Contact Center → Call Logs for full recordings, transcripts and outcomes of every connected call — including which agent took it.
  • The session's own tallies (connects, voicemails, abandoned, AI handoffs) stay visible on the card until you start the next run.

That's the whole loop: load a list in the morning, flip your team Online, and let the Human Dialer keep their headsets warm all day. 🚀

metaTitle: Human Dialer — Power & Predictive Dialing | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Set up the iMash Human Dialer: build a dial list from your CRM, choose Power Dial or Predictive mode, configure AI answering-machine screening, route connected humans to your agent group, and run live server-side dial sessions.
